metadata.name: Traefik Buffering Middleware
metadata.description: Traefik Middleware CRD for buffering request and response bodies so uploads, downloads, and upstream size controls behave more predictably.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
Creates a Traefik Middleware resource for `buffering` so request and response body limits are enforced close to the edge.

## Prerequisites
- Traefik must be installed with the Kubernetes CRD provider enabled.
- Confirm the chosen size limits match both your application and any upstream reverse proxies in front of Traefik.

## Apply
- Render `main.yaml`, review the request and response body limits, then apply it with `kubectl apply -f main.yaml`.
- Attach the middleware to one route and test upload and download behavior before using the same settings broadly.

## Follow-Up
- Keep the configured limits aligned with app-level upload limits so clients get consistent behavior.
- Increase in-memory thresholds carefully because they directly influence edge memory usage.

## References
- [Buffering Middleware](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/middlewares/http/buffering/)
- [Traefik Kubernetes CRD](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/routing/providers/kubernetes-crd/)

items.name: resource_name
items.title: Middleware name
items.description: Name to use for the Middleware resource.

items.name: max_request_body_bytes
items.title: Max request body bytes
items.description: Maximum allowed request body size in bytes.

items.name: mem_request_body_bytes
items.title: In-memory request body bytes
items.description: Maximum request body size held in memory before Traefik uses disk buffering.

items.name: max_response_body_bytes
items.title: Max response body bytes
items.description: Maximum allowed response body size in bytes.

items.name: mem_response_body_bytes
items.title: In-memory response body bytes
items.description: Maximum response body size held in memory before Traefik uses disk buffering.
